data.title: REG - Tower Bridge 2023-2 - Notice to Note Holders
data.description:
RNS Number : 6968B Tower Bridge Funding 2023-2 PLC 21 March 2025  

CONFIRMATION OF REDEMPTION OF NOTES AND CANCELLATION OF LISTING

Tower Bridge Funding 2023-2 PLC

(Registered in England and Wales under number 14762325)

(the "Issuer")

Class A Regulation S Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624941360

Class A Rule 144A Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624941527

Class B Regulation S Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624941790

Class B Rule 144A Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624941873

Class C Regulation S Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624942095

Class C Rule 144A Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624942178

Class D Regulation S Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624942418

Class D Rule 144A Notes due March 2065 ISIN: XS2624942509

Class S Regulation S Notes due March 2065

Class Z1 Regulation S Notes due March 2065

Class Z2 Regulation S Notes due March 2065 (the "Notes")

The Master Definitions Schedule, dated 19 June 2023 and signed for the purpose of identification by Cadwalader, Wickersham & Taft LLP and Allen Overy Shearman Sterling LLP (formerly Allen & Overy LLP) in connection with the Notes (as the same may have been amended, varied or supplemented from time to time, the "Master Definitions Schedule") is expressly and specifically incorporated into this notice and, accordingly, the expressions defined in the Master Definitions Schedule shall, except where the context otherwise requires and save where otherwise defined in this notice, have the same meanings in this notice and this notice shall be construed in accordance with the interpretation provisions set out in Clause 2 (Interpretation) of the Master Definitions Schedule.

NOTICE IS GIVEN by the Issuer to each Noteholder in accordance with Note Condition 13 (Notice to Noteholders) that the Issuer has fully redeemed all of the Notes on 20 March 2025, and that the Financial Conduct Authority as competent authority under the UK Prospectus Regulation has cancelled the listing of the applicable Notes on the Official List of the Financial Conduct Authority with effect from 21 March 2025 and the London Stock Exchange plc has removed the admission of the applicable Notes to trading on London Stock Exchange plc's main market with effect from 21 March 2025.

By:  Tower Bridge Funding 2023-2 PLC

10th Floor, 5 Churchill Place

London E14 5HU

as Issuer

Dated 21 March 2025

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. data.title: As stocks wobble, these two rarely-noticed measures say valuations are stretched
data.description:
By Jamie Chisholm

The relationships between earnings, inflation and money supply show elevated exuberance

U.S. stocks continue to be choppy, with intraday ranges of 100 points for S&P 500 futures (ES00) not uncommon of late.

The moves come as concerns build that overvalued stocks may look more so if the current policy uncertainty badly damages the economy and hits corporate earnings.

Troy Ludtka, senior U.S. economist at SMBC Nikko Securities Americas, considers two rarely observed measures that illustrate just how richly valued the broader stock market may be.

The first is what he calls "one of our preferred yet simple macro frameworks" which plots earnings multiples and core inflation.

Historically, the two have been tightly and inversely correlated, he notes. However, since the COVID pandemic, earnings multiples have become completely detached from the relationship, to an extent that has never happened before.

"We find ourselves most interested in how a return to this underpinning is achieved as inflation and earnings expectations are quite high," says Ludtka.

He says the most painless scenario, and the one that has been unfolding of late, requires inflation slowing to about the 2% target without an accompanying decrease in earnings. That would be shown as a leftward move in the chart below.

"Yet, if this current leftward trajectory continues, U.S. markets will soon enter territory only charted in the IT bubble! In other words, if inflation comes down alone, history suggests we are looking at irrational exuberance," he says.

And if price-to-earnings multiples fall, then the economy could face negative wealth effects and a possible downturn because, he reckons, U.S. stocks are over-owned.

The second measure observes that the equity market exuberance appears to be corroborated by the ratio of the S&P 500 to the M2 measure of the money supply, with the latest data showing the highest reading since the fourth quarter of 2000, according to Ludtka.

"Further, U.S. stocks are expensive and over-owned which can be toxic if combined with periods of heightened uncertainty and elevated expectations," he says.

"Now, this is not to say that stocks cannot continue to perform well in the short run, because they can, but theory suggests that longer run performance could suffer," Ludtka concludes.

Markets

U.S. stock-index futures (ES00) (YM00) (NQ00) are lower as benchmark Treasury yields BX:TMUBMUSD10Y dip. The dollar index DXY is higher, while oil prices (CL.1) fall and gold (GC00) is trading around $3,032 an ounce.

   Key asset performance                                                Last       5d      1m      YTD     1y 
   S&P 500                                                              5662.89    0.42%   -5.82%  -3.72%  8.19% 
   Nasdaq Composite                                                     17,691.63  -0.35%  -9.39%  -8.38%  7.69% 
   10-year Treasury                                                     4.224      -9.30   -20.60  -35.20  2.20 
   Gold                                                                 3037.5     1.47%   2.98%   15.09%  40.20% 
   Oil                                                                  68.03      1.25%   -3.15%  -5.34%  -15.81% 
   Data: MarketWatch. Treasury yields change expressed in basis points

The buzz

Wall Street faces a $4.7 trillion 'triple-witching' option expiration session on Friday.

London's Heathrow airport is closed all Friday, with inbound flights re-rerouted, after a fire caused a power outage.

Elon Musk held an all-hands meeting with Tesla employees at which he said the company would build 5,000 Optimus robots this year and he urged staff not to sell their shares (TSLA).

FedEx shares (FDX) are lower after the parcel shipper became the latest company to warn about weakness and uncertainty in the U.S. economy.

Micron Technology shares (MU) are up in premarket action after the memory -chip maker's fiscal second quarter showed it is still seeing a boost from AI.

Nike stock (NKE) is falling after the sneaker giant said the current quarter could take a hit from the companies turnaround efforts.

Best of the web

One man's crypto windfall Is funding a $1 billion space station dream.

Europe is short of gunpowder and TNT when it needs them most.

How a renowned Hong Kong tycoon ended up on Beijing's bad side over Panama Canal deal.

The chart

There is currently a lot of talk about how soft economic data - such as consumer sentiment surveys - are much more pessimistic that hard data, such as retail sales. This may be explained by the fact that people are having to borrow or dip into savings to buy what they want or need. The spending still takes place, but people are not happy about it.

With that in mind, the chart below from Torsten Slok, Apollo chief economist, shows that households' ability to come up with $2,000 for an emergency expense within the next month is at the lowest level since the survey started in fourth quarter of 2015. "Taking into account that the CPI level today is 35% higher than in 2015, the situation is even worse," says Slok.

Top tickers

Here were the most active stock-market tickers on MarketWatch as of 6 a.m. Eastern.

   Ticker  Security name 
   TSLA    Tesla 
   NVDA    Nvidia 
   GME     GameStop 
   NIO     NIO 
   PLTR    Palantir Technologies 
   TSM     Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing 
   MU      Micron Technology 
   AAPL    Apple 
   QBTS    D-Wave Quantum 
   NKE     Nike

Random reads

Meme stonks come to Europe. A pictorial odyssey.

Farewell to Walter, the friendly alligator.

Animal 'poo zoo' to protect endangered species.

-Jamie Chisholm

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Shareholders that lost money on Monolithic Power Systems Inc.(MPWR) Urged to Join Class Action - Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Learn More
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 21, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Monolithic Power Systems Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/monolithic-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137661&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Monolithic Power Systems Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 8, 2024 and November 8, 2024.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (a) Monolithic's voltage regulator modules and power management integrated circuits were suffering from significant performance and quality control issues; (b) the defects listed in (a), above, had, in turn, negatively impacted the performance of certain products offered by the Company's largest customer, Nvidia, in which such products were used; (c) Monolithic had failed to adequately address and resolve known issues affecting the performance of the power management solutions the Company supplied to Nvidia; (d) Monolithic's relationship with Nvidia had been irreparably damaged due to the significant performance and quality control.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Monolithic stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/monolithic-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137661&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.description:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/fluence-energy-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137656&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Fluence Energy, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 29, 2023 and February 10, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) Fluence's relationship with its founders and largest sources of revenue, Siemens AG and The AES Corporation, was poised to decline; (2) Siemens Energy, Siemens AG's U.S. affiliate, had accused the Company of engineering failures and fraud; (3) Fluence's margins and revenue growth were inflated as Siemens and AES were moving to divest; and (4) based on the foregoing, defendants lacked a reasonable basis for their positive statements related to Fluence's battery energy storage business, as well as related financial results, growth, and prospects.

WHAT'S NEXT? data.title: Sempra Being Investigated on Behalf of Sempra Investors. Contact Levi & Korsinsky For Details.
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 21, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Sempra ("Sempra") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Before the market opened, Sempra released its fourth-quarter and full-year 2024 financial results on February 25, 2025, reporting annual revenue of $3.76 billion-falling $970 million short of consensus estimates. The company also lowered its 2025 earnings guidance to a midpoint of $4.50 per share, down from the previous $5.00 per share outlook. Sempra attributed the disappointing results to "higher interest and operating expenses and lower consumption primarily due to mild weather."

Following this news, Sempra's stock price dropped over 19% on the same day, causing investor losses. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/sempra-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137635&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Lost Money on Atkore Inc.(ATKR)? Join Class Action Suit Seeking Recovery - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 21, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Atkore Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/atkore-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137651&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Atkore Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between August 2, 2022 and February 3, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) Atkore engaged in an anticompetitive price-fixing scheme that artificially inflated the price of PVC Pipes; (2) Atkore reaped significant, unsustainable financial benefits from its anticompetitive conduct; (3) as Atkore's price-fixing scheme was exposed, the Company and its price-fixing co-conspirators were no longer able to artificially inflate the price of PVC Pipes, resulting in a substantial decrease in the price of PVC Pipes; (4) Atkore's business and operations were negatively impacted; and (5) as a result, defendants' positive statements Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially false and misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Atkore Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/atkore-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137651&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

data.title: LPRO ALERT- Levi & Korsinsky Has Commenced an Investigation on Behalf of Open Lending Corporation Shareholders Who Lost Money
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 21, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Open Lending Corporation ("Open Lending Corporation") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

The company announced a delay in filing its annual Form 10-K and postponed its Q4 and full-year 2024 earnings release. Open Lending cited the need for additional time to finalize its financial statements, raising concerns about the company's internal controls and financial reporting.

Following this news, shares of Open Lending Corp. fell over 11% in intraday trading on March 17, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/open-lending-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137638&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. data.title: Veon Partners With Airspan Networks for Ukraine's Communications Infrastructure
data.description:
Veon said Friday it has agreed with Airspan Networks to explore resilient network solutions in Ukraine,

The partnership will focus on evaluating technical and commercial synergies, including Open RAN and air-to-land technologies, to improve Ukraine's communications infrastructure amid extreme conditions.

Veon said the partnership is part of Veon's $1 billion investment commitment to rebuild Ukraine's digital infrastructure between 2023 and 2027.

data.title: Correction: Caledonia Mining Names Ross Jerrard as CFO
data.description:
(Corrects the headline to say the company appointed Ross Jerrard as CFO.)

Caledonia Mining said Wednesday it has appointed Ross Jerrard as chief financial officer, succeeding Chester Goodburn.

Goodburn will step down from the role March 24. He will then assume a consultancy role to assist with the transition, the company said.

data.title: Correction to Advance Auto Parts Earnings Article on Feb. 26
data.description: Advance Auto Parts reported a fourth-quarter loss of $6.92 a share. "Advance Auto Parts 2025 Outlook Beats Wall Street Views," published at 6:49 a.m. ET on Feb. 26, incorrectly said the loss was $10.16 a share. The error also appeared in the headline "Advance Auto Parts 4Q Loss/Shr $10.16 >AAP," published at 6:30 a.m. ET on Feb. 26.

data.title: Copper falls on stronger dollar, Comex premium near record high
data.description:
Copper prices fell on Friday under pressure from a stronger dollar, while mounting worries that the United States may impose new import tariffs on the metal kept the New York futures premium over the London price near the record high.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) fell 0.9% to $9,845 a metric ton by 1103 GMT. The contract hit its five-month high of $10,046.50 on Thursday.

Most active copper futures on the U.S. Comex exchange (HGc3) were last down 1.2% at $5.049 a pound. They hit a 10-month high on Thursday.

About a month ago, U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a probe into potential new tariffs on copper imports with the aim to rebuild U.S. production of the metal. Technically, the copper probe can take up to nine months.

"Supply concerns over potential tariffs have played out on the CME-LME spread, with the U.S. heavily reliant on copper imports," said Natalie Scott-Gray, senior metals analyst at StoneX.

U.S. imports about 40% of its needs in copper, including from Canada and Mexico, targeted by Trump's tariff policy. Trump's 25% tariffs on steel and aluminium products took effect last week. Trump has said that his "reciprocal tariffs" to bring U.S. tariffs to other countries' levels will take effect on April 2.

The premium of the Comex contract over the LME one hit a record high of $1,346 per ton on Thursday and was last at $1,290, or 13%. StoneX estimates that it would rise to $2,000 if the U.S. decides to impose 25% copper tariffs.

The incentive to deliver copper to the U.S. ahead of possible tariffs has also resulted in some trade re-routing with traders swapping the LME-deliverable copper with producers and consumers for CME-deliverable brands, StoneX added.

The on-warrant copper stocks in the LME system fell to 117,775 tons, the lowest since June, after 8,200 tons of fresh cancellations, daily LME data showed. Cancelled stocks currently represent 48% of the LME's total copper stocks. (0#MCUSTX-LOC)

LME aluminium fell 1.0% to $2,631.50 a ton, zinc lost 0.4% to $2,905, lead was down 0.9% at $2,038.50, tin dropped 1.0% to $34,920, while nickel slid 0.7% to $16,165.

data.title: Share Buy-back programme at SP Group A/S
data.description:
Announcement No. 12 / 202521 March 2025CVR No. 15701315

Share buy-back programme at SP Group A/S

On 26 August 2024, SP Group initiated a share buy-back programme, cf. company announcement no. 12/2024 of 23 August 2024.

According to the programme, SP Group will in the period from 26 August 2024 until 10 April 2025 purchase own shares for a maximum amount of DKK 40.0 million.

The share buy-back programme was initiated and structured in compliance with the EU Commission Regulation No. 596/2014 of 16 April 2014 about Market Abuse, the so-called Market Abuse Regulation that protects listed companies’ board and executive board against violation of insider legislation in connection with share buy-backs.

The following transactions have been made under the programme:

As of today, SP Group’s total holding of own shares is 479,068 number of shares of a nominal value of DKK 2, corresponding to 3.84 % of the total number of issued shares of 12,490,000.

For further information:

CEO Lars Bering

Phone: +45 70 23 23 79

www.sp-group.dk

In case of any discrepancies, the Danish version shall prevail.

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 attached?
  • NO
Date of disclosure21/03/2025
Contact nameJames Carr
Telephone number+442071447242

Public disclosures under Rule 8.3 of the Rules must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

Ap31

NOTES ON FORM 8.3

1.      See the definition of “connected fund manager” in Rule 2.2 of Part A of the Rules.

2.      See the definition of “interest in a relevant security” in Rule 2.5 of Part A of the Rules and see Rule 8.6(a) and (b) of Part B of the Rules.

3.      See the definition of “relevant securities” in Rule 2.1 of Part A of the Rules.

4.      See the definition of “dealing” in Rule 2.1 of Part A of the Rules.

5.      If the economic exposure to changes in the price of securities is limited, for example, by virtue of a stop loss arrangement relating to a spread bet, full details must be given.

6.      See Rule 2.5(d) of Part A of the Rules.

7.      If details included in a disclosure under Rule 8 are incorrect, they should be corrected as soon as practicable in a subsequent disclosure. Such disclosure should state clearly that it corrects details disclosed previously, identify the disclosure or disclosures being corrected, and provide sufficient detail for the reader to understand the nature of the corrections. In the case of any doubt, the Panel should be consulted.

For full details of disclosure requirements, see Rule 8 of the Rules. If in doubt, consult the Panel.

References in these notes to “the Rules” are to the Irish Takeover Panel Act, 1997, Takeover Rules, 2022.
